pointers.Lookup_func: engine/btree/lookup.go: func Lookup(store *NodeStore, rootNodeID uint64, path string) (~lines 90-119)
pointers.test_scaffolding_tree_builder: engine/btree/lookup_test.go: func buildTestTree (~lines 29-96) -- explicitly commented as TEST-ONLY SCAFFOLDING, NOT subtask 1.2.3's real insert-with-splitting API

node_id_to_offset_addressing_convention.formula: byte offset = nodeID * NodeSize (NodeSize=4096, defined in engine/btree/node.go)
node_id_to_offset_addressing_convention.reserved_id: node ID 0 is reserved and never valid (mirrors node.go's noSibling sentinel and engine/catalog/file.go's reserved free-list page 0); real node IDs start at 1
node_id_to_offset_addressing_convention.precedent: directly analogous to engine/catalog/file.go's FileManager pageID * PageSize convention
node_id_to_offset_addressing_convention.scope_note: NodeStore has NO allocator/free-list (unlike catalog's FileManager) -- it only reads/writes nodes at caller-chosen IDs. An allocator, if ever needed, is deferred to whichever later subtask's LLD calls for it (likely folded into 1.2.3's real insert).

internal_node_traversal_rule: sort.Search for the first key strictly greater than the target path in InternalNode.Keys; descend into Children[i] (the child immediately before that key), per the covering convention already documented on InternalNode in node.go: Children[i] covers keys < Keys[i] for i==0, keys in [Keys[i-1], Keys[i]) for interior children, keys >= Keys[n-1] for the last child.
test_scaffolding_explicit_note: buildTestTree in lookup_test.go is TEST-ONLY SCAFFOLDING to exercise Lookup. It hand-constructs a fixed, pre-balanced 3-level tree (no auto-splitting/rebalancing) and is explicitly NOT subtask 1.2.3's real insert API -- do not reuse or mistake it for that deliverable.
acceptance_criteria: Lookup returns the correct fileID for an inserted path; returns a well-defined not-found result (found=false, err=nil) for an absent path.
not_done: No self-verification performed beyond internal self-consistency (build/vet/test green, validation matrix covered) -- per protocol, a separate /cdr:verify pass follows
No node-splitting insert (1.2.3), delete (1.2.4), or prefix scan (1.2.5) -- explicitly out of scope
No latch-crabbing / optimistic-read concurrency logic -- deferred to a later concurrency-focused subtask per docs/LLD/btree.md
